title: Green Kickstart Smoothie
intro: A bright, leafy blend that tastes far greener than it drinks. This is the kind of fuss-free glass that turns a rushed morning into a reset.
ingredients: 1 ripe green apple, cored and chopped
steps: Add the spinach, coconut water and lemon juice to the blender and blitz until smooth and no leaf flecks remain.
Drop in the apple, banana, celery and cucumber.
Blend on high until completely creamy, about 45 seconds.
Toss in the ice and pulse a few times to chill and loosen the texture.
Pour into two tall glasses and serve right away.
tip: Blend the greens with the liquid first for a silky drink with no stringy bits.

title: Sunrise Mango Lassi
intro: Thick, cooling and gently spiced, this golden yogurt drink is pure sunshine in a glass. It is the perfect partner for anything fiery on the table.
ingredients: 1 large ripe mango, peeled and cubed
steps: Pile the mango, yogurt and milk into the blender.
Add the honey and cardamom.
Blend on high until thick and velvety, about 30 seconds.
Drop in the ice and blend again briefly to chill.
Pour into two glasses and dust with a little extra cardamom.
tip: Use very ripe, fragrant mango so you need barely any added sweetness.

title: Classic Fresh Lemonade
intro: Crisp, tart and just sweet enough, this is homemade lemonade the way it should taste. A simple syrup base means every sip is perfectly balanced.
ingredients: 3/4 cup fresh lemon juice, about 4 lemons
steps: Warm the sugar and half cup of water in a small pan, stirring until the sugar fully dissolves, then cool.
Squeeze and strain the fresh lemon juice into a large jug.
Pour in the cooled syrup and the cold water and stir well.
Taste and adjust with a splash more water or a little more syrup.
Fill two glasses with ice, add lemon slices and pour over the lemonade.
tip: Roll the lemons firmly on the counter before juicing to release more liquid.

title: Rich Spiced Hot Chocolate
intro: Deep, glossy and unashamedly indulgent, this hot chocolate is more dessert than drink. A whisper of cinnamon and vanilla makes it feel like a warm hug.
steps: Warm the milk in a saucepan over medium heat until steaming but not boiling.
Whisk in the cocoa powder, sugar and cinnamon until smooth.
Add the chopped chocolate and whisk gently until fully melted and glossy.
Stir in the vanilla and let it bubble softly for one minute to thicken.
Pour into two mugs and serve hot.
tip: Chop the chocolate finely so it melts evenly without leaving lumps.

title: Virgin Watermelon Mojito
intro: A refreshing riot of watermelon, lime and mint with all the zip and none of the alcohol. This is the mocktail everyone reaches for on a hot afternoon.
ingredients: 12 fresh mint leaves, plus more to serve
steps: Blend the watermelon until smooth, then strain through a sieve into a jug.
Muddle the mint leaves with the lime juice and honey in the bottom of the jug.
Pour in the strained watermelon juice and stir to combine.
Fill two glasses with ice and divide the mixture between them.
Top each with sparkling water and garnish with fresh mint.
tip: Muddle the mint just enough to release its oils; over-crushing turns it bitter.

title: Iced Vanilla Latte
intro: Smooth cold espresso softened with milk and a thread of vanilla syrup. It is the cafe-style pick-me-up you can pull together in minutes at home.
ingredients: 1 cup strong brewed espresso or coffee, cooled
steps: Brew the espresso or strong coffee and let it cool to room temperature.
Stir the vanilla syrup and optional sugar into the coffee until dissolved.
Fill two tall glasses generously with ice.
Pour the sweetened coffee over the ice, dividing evenly.
Top each glass with cold milk and stir gently before serving.
tip: Freeze leftover coffee into ice cubes so your latte never gets watered down.

title: Berry Almond Breakfast Smoothie
intro: A ruby-hued blend of mixed berries and creamy almond milk that drinks like a treat. Naturally sweet and satisfying, it is breakfast you can sip on the run.
steps: Add the frozen berries, banana and almond milk to the blender.
Spoon in the almond butter, honey and vanilla.
Blend on high until thick and smooth, about one minute.
Loosen with a splash more almond milk if it is too thick to pour.
Divide between two glasses and serve chilled.
tip: Frozen berries keep the smoothie cold and thick without needing extra ice.

title: Mint Cucumber Cooler
intro: Clean, herbal and wonderfully thirst-quenching, this pale-green cooler tastes like a garden in a glass. A squeeze of lime keeps every sip lively and fresh.
steps: Blend the cucumber, mint, lime juice, honey and cold water until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a jug, pressing to extract the juice.
Taste and adjust with a little more honey or lime if needed.
Fill two glasses with ice and pour in the cucumber blend.
Top each with sparkling water and stir once before serving.
tip: Keep the cooler in the fridge and add the sparkling water only just before serving.

title: Fresh Carrot Ginger Juice
intro: A vivid orange juice with a warming spark of ginger and a hit of citrus. Bright and energizing, it is the glass that makes healthy feel genuinely delicious.
steps: Chop the carrots, oranges and apple into pieces that fit your juicer chute.
Feed the carrots and ginger through the juicer first.
Follow with the apple, oranges and lemon.
Stir the juice well, as the layers can separate.
Pour over ice in two glasses and drink fresh.
tip: Drink the juice within an hour or two, since fresh juice loses brightness quickly.

title: Iced Peach Green Tea
intro: Delicately grassy green tea sweetened with ripe peach and a touch of honey. Served over ice, it is the most refreshing thing to sip through a lazy afternoon.
steps: Steep the green tea bags in the hot water for three minutes, then remove them.
Stir the honey into the warm tea until fully dissolved, then let it cool.
Muddle half the peach slices with the lemon juice in a jug.
Pour in the cooled tea and stir to combine.
Fill two glasses with ice, add the remaining peach slices and pour over the tea.
tip: Do not over-steep green tea or it turns bitter; three minutes is plenty.

title: Pineapple Coconut Smoothie
intro: A creamy tropical escape of sweet pineapple and mellow coconut in every sip. Thick, frosty and vibrant, it tastes like a beach holiday in a glass.
steps: Add the frozen pineapple, banana and coconut milk to the blender.
Spoon in the yogurt, honey and lime juice.
Blend on high until thick and creamy, about one minute.
Add a splash of water if you prefer a thinner texture.
Pour into two glasses and serve immediately.
tip: A squeeze of lime lifts the sweetness and stops the smoothie tasting flat.

title: Sparkling Pomegranate Mocktail
intro: Jewel-red, fizzy and just tart enough, this festive mocktail feels like a celebration. Fresh lime and mint keep it crisp and grown-up without any alcohol.
steps: Whisk the pomegranate juice, lime juice and honey together in a jug until blended.
Fill two glasses with ice.
Divide the pomegranate mixture between the glasses.
Top each with chilled sparkling water and stir gently.
Scatter in the pomegranate seeds and finish with a sprig of mint.
tip: Chill the sparkling water well so the mocktail stays fizzy right to the last sip.
